title: By The River.
content:
Flow on, ye lays so loved, so fair,

    On to Oblivion's ocean flow!
    May no rapt boy recall you e'er,

    No maiden in her beauty's glow!

    My love alone was then your theme,

    But now she scorns my passion true.
    Ye were but written in the stream;

    As it flows on, then, flow ye too!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, titled "By The River.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
